Gender Prediction from Tweets: Improving Neural Representations with Hand-Crafted Features

Erhan Sezerer

Dept. of Computer Engineering ˙Izmir Institute of Technology ˙Izmir, Turkey erhansezerer@iyte.edu.tr

Ozan Polatbilek

Dept.

b8dc0b18-9fb3-4dd1-85f7-d4b74e0e4044.label: text_chunk
b8dc0b18-9fb3-4dd1-85f7-d4b74e0e4044.text:
of Computer Engineering ˙Izmir, Turkey ozanpolatbilek@iyte.edu.tr

Selma Tekir

Dept. of Computer Engineering ˙Izmir, Turkey selmatekir@iyte.edu.tr

Abstract

Author profiling is the characterization of an author through some key attributes such as gender, age, and language.

6aebe0c3-d600-4aaf-991e-10e31854e25e.label: text_chunk
6aebe0c3-d600-4aaf-991e-10e31854e25e.text:
In this paper, a RNN model with Attention (RNNwA) is proposed to predict the gender of a twitter user using their tweets. Both word level and tweet level attentions are utilized to learn ’where to look’. This model 1 is improved by concatenating LSA-reduced n-gram features with the learned neural representation of a user.

 Both models are tested on three languages: English, Spanish, Arabic.

7afa16d5-2d94-4dc7-b8d2-d3de430bf486.label: text_chunk
7afa16d5-2d94-4dc7-b8d2-d3de430bf486.text:
The improved version of the proposed model (RNNwA + n-gram) achieves state-of-the-art performance on English and has competitive results on Spanish and Arabic.

1 Introduction

Author profiling is the characterization of an author through some key attributes such as gender, age, and language. It’s an indispensable task especially in security, forensics, and marketing. Recently, social media has become a great data source for the potential learning approaches.

29d301a6-2640-4abf-8523-70c20b653647.label: text_chunk
29d301a6-2640-4abf-8523-70c20b653647.text:
Furthermore, gender prediction has been a popular profiling task.The traditional approach to gender prediction problem is extracting a useful set of hand-crafted features and then feeding them into a standard classification algorithm.

 In their study, Kucukyilmaz et al. (2006) work with the style-based features of message length, stop word usage, frequency of smiley etc.

464f8eb6-276c-41c7-84bb-8a77de77adb5.label: text_chunk
464f8eb6-276c-41c7-84bb-8a77de77adb5.text:
and use different classifiers such as k-nearest neighbor, naive bayes, covering rules, and backpropagation to predict gender on chat messages. Similarly, Deitrick et al. (2012) select some hand-crafted features and feed them into various classifiers.

Most of the work on gender prediction rely on n-gram features (Miller et al., 2012).

54f58c56-cb1a-404a-9850-fd6565462ff8.label: text_chunk
54f58c56-cb1a-404a-9850-fd6565462ff8.text: Daneshvar and Inkpen (2018) give Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA)-reduced forms of word and character n- grams into Support Vector Machine (SVM) and achieve state-of-the-art performance. Apart from exploiting n-gram frequencies, there are studies (Ljubeˇsi´c et al.

4918ce16-4075-4654-861c-d2719cdd0335.label: text_chunk
4918ce16-4075-4654-861c-d2719cdd0335.text: , 2017), (Alowibdi et al., 2013), (van der Goot et al., 2018) to extract cross-lingual features to determine gender from tweets. Some other work (Ljubeˇsi´c et al., 2017), (Sayyadiharikandeh et al., 2016) exploit user metadata besides using just tweets.1 https://github.

9478bf1a-db82-4cd0-86fc-94a5e04c4c82.label: text_chunk
9478bf1a-db82-4cd0-86fc-94a5e04c4c82.text:
com/Darg-Iztech/gender-prediction-from-tweets
Recently, neural network-based models have been proposed to solve this problem. Rather than explicitly extracting features, the aim is to develop an architecture that implicitly learns. In author profiling, both style and content-based features were proved useful (Argamon et al., 2009) and neural networks are able to capture both syntactic and semantic regularities.

45dbf0c1-91bf-4e35-859f-c577a21bc8ba.label: text_chunk
45dbf0c1-91bf-4e35-859f-c577a21bc8ba.text: In general, syntactic informa- tion is drawn from the local context. On the other hand, semantic information is often captured with larger window sizes. Thus, CNNs are preferred to obtain style-based features while RNNs are the methods of choice for addressing content-based features (Goldberg, 2017). In literature, CNN (Sez- erer et al., 2018) or RNN (Takahashi et al.

712721b5-dad1-4c3b-8373-81a81dbb75b2.label: text_chunk
712721b5-dad1-4c3b-8373-81a81dbb75b2.text: , 2018), (Kodiyan et al., 2017), (Sezerer et al., 2019) is used on this task. Takahashi et al. (2018) obtain state-of-the-art performance among neural methods by proposing a model architecture where they process text through RNN with GRU cells. Also, the presence of an attention layer is shown to boost the performance of neural methods (Takahashi et al.

470733a5-f566-4880-b742-e52b4fdd15ff.label: text_chunk
470733a5-f566-4880-b742-e52b4fdd15ff.text: , 2018), (Sezerer et al., 2018).In this work, we propose a model that relies on RNN with attention mechanism (RNNwA). A bidi- rectional RNN with attention mechanism both on word level and tweet level is trained with word embeddings. The final representation of the user is fed to a fully connected layer for prediction.

49245c80-1e74-4d73-bbc6-64fff5bb97dc.label: text_chunk
49245c80-1e74-4d73-bbc6-64fff5bb97dc.text: Since combining some hand-crafted features with a learned linear layer has shown to perform well in complex tasks like Semantic Role Labeling (SRL) (Collobert and Weston, 2008), an improved version of the model (RNNwA + n-gram) is also tested with hand-crafted features. In the improved version, LSA-reduced n-gram features are concatenated with the neural representation of the user.

dc625e38-1083-48cd-9484-6f0a87b86c59.label: text_chunk
dc625e38-1083-48cd-9484-6f0a87b86c59.text: Then the result is fed into a fully-connected layer to make prediction. Models are tested in three lan- guages; English, Spanish, and Arabic, and the improved version achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on English, and competitive results on Spanish and Arabic corpus.There are many datasets created for this task (Pardo et al., 2018), (Sezerer et al., 2019).

9271dc90-b0ca-4223-a212-306458c31e72.label: text_chunk
9271dc90-b0ca-4223-a212-306458c31e72.text: In this work, we have used the dataset and benchmarks provided by the PAN 2018 shared task on author profiling (Pardo et al., 2018). As the dataset contains a constant number of 100 tweets per user, accuracy tests are performed both on user and tweet level (tweet-level predictions are made by removing the user-level attention). Tweet-level accuracy tests show interesting results during hyperparameter optimization.

bbb8b5dc-7381-4b17-a181-86a316b2443f.label: text_chunk
bbb8b5dc-7381-4b17-a181-86a316b2443f.text: When the tweet-level predictions are averaged to produce user-level predictions, it is seen that the hyperparameters that gave the best results in terms of tweet-level accuracy, performs worse in user-level accuracy. The better user-level models, with different hyperparameters, that gave the highest user-level accuracy are observed to slightly overfit on tweet-level.

844f6b5d-123d-472a-8977-2a8d8fb77dc6.label: text_chunk
844f6b5d-123d-472a-8977-2a8d8fb77dc6.text:
It leads us to believe that the overfitting in the tweet-level predictions in best user-level models acts similar to an attention mechanism by over-emphasizing some distinctive tweets and ignoring the rest.

2 Model architecture

In author profiling, both style-based and content-based features must be addressed (Argamon et al., 2009). An appropriate baseline for this task is a CNN-based model that is able to capture style-based information (Sezerer et al., 2018).

0bcf8bed-5ac3-4674-9047-29c044f9cf9a.label: text_chunk
0bcf8bed-5ac3-4674-9047-29c044f9cf9a.text:
The proposed RNN-based model relies on extracting content- based features. In addition, in order to improve its accuracy, the proposed model is combined with some hand-crafted features.

 For all of the models, Adam optimizer (Kingma and Ba, 2014) is used with cross-entropy loss along with the L2 regularization to prevent from overfitting.

8a40704f-bd1f-4090-aae3-ea548e0d4587.label: text_chunk
8a40704f-bd1f-4090-aae3-ea548e0d4587.text:
2.1 Baseline CNN model

CNN model (denoted CNNwA on results) is based on Sezerer et al. (2018) where each character in the tweet is represented with a character embedding of size 25 , which is trained along the neural network. All characters are lower-cased.

f03a7976-ec79-4da1-9f9f-2c61a05a7aee.label: text_chunk
f03a7976-ec79-4da1-9f9f-2c61a05a7aee.text:
Non-alphabetical characters such as punctuation are kept with a view to capturing some information on the profile of the user since they are heavily used in twitter as emoticons.

Filters of size 3 × 3 , 6 × 6 and 9 × 9 are used for each language, and the number of filters is determined by performing grid search on validation set.

67b5006e-f7fc-404a-b5c6-1df6f98df617.label: text_chunk
67b5006e-f7fc-404a-b5c6-1df6f98df617.text:
Among the tested range ( 50 - 125 with intervals of 25 ), the number of filters that gives the best accuracy is 100 (per each filter), for all languages.2
Figure 1: Proposed model.

d23b1e7e-7575-4bff-90c1-7d1bdffed280.label: text_chunk
d23b1e7e-7575-4bff-90c1-7d1bdffed280.text:
2.2 RNN Model

Since the dataset is not big enough to train word embeddings, Glove word embeddings (Pennington et al., 2014) of size 200 are used in the proposed RNN Model (denoted RNNwA on results) due to their success at various NLP tasks and their multi-linguality: They encompass all the languages in the test set.

5ea7972b-b19e-4371-9816-225d2a1e448a.label: text_chunk
5ea7972b-b19e-4371-9816-225d2a1e448a.text: In addition, the Glove embeddings are also trained on Twitter data which make them reflect the nature of the dataset better than other alternatives.A bidirectional RNN with GRU (Chung et al., 2014) cells are used in this model where the number of cells is a hyperparameter. Among the tested range ( 50 - 150 with intervals of 25 ), best accuracy on validation set is obtained by 150 cells in English and 100 cells in Spanish and Arabic.

57099170-e59d-479b-99b4-10d962fdb632.label: text_chunk
57099170-e59d-479b-99b4-10d962fdb632.text: An attention mechanism is used on word-level in addition to tweet-level to capture the important parts of each tweet as shown in Figure 1.A feature vector for each tweet is created by feeding tweets to RNN separately. In order to discrim- inate tweets with respect to their information carrying capacity on its author’s gender, Bahdanau attention mechanism (Bahdanau et al.

ffd72855-a89b-4da9-be5b-0db1e70fdd51.label: text_chunk
ffd72855-a89b-4da9-be5b-0db1e70fdd51.text:
, 2014) is used to combine the tweets rather than concate- nating them before feeding to the network or averaging their predictions later. Figure 2 shows the tweet-level attention layer in detail which is calculated by the following formulas:
3
Figure 2: Tweet-level Attention Layer in Detail.

3db48b68-8839-450e-a4b4-55a890db9439.label: text_chunk
3db48b68-8839-450e-a4b4-55a890db9439.text: where W α is a learnable weight matrix that is used to multiply each output of the RNN, t i is the feature vector of i th tweet, b is a learnable bias vector, w i is a learnable attention weight, A i is the attention context vector, v i is the attention value for i th tweet, o i is attention output vector for the corresponding tweet, K is the output vector for user.

5d0d2cb6-b11a-48bb-ad58-0f610bebabbd.label: text_chunk
5d0d2cb6-b11a-48bb-ad58-0f610bebabbd.text:
Matrix W α and vectors w i and b are learned parameters.

Attention layer outputs a single feature vector that corresponds to a user, which is then fed to a fully-connected layer to lower the dimension to the number of classes.There are two different attention layers on the model. One is a word level attention where it amplifies the signal coming from important words, the other one is on tweet level where it combines the signals coming from each tweet and creates the final representation of a user.4

2.3 RNN with N-gram Model

For this model (denoted RNNwA + n-gram on results), n-gram features are collected with the same method described in Daneshvar and Inkpen (2018).

be2657b9-cf8a-43be-a762-f12fa67a2887.label: text_chunk
be2657b9-cf8a-43be-a762-f12fa67a2887.text: At the beginning, word level and character level n-gram features are obtained and concatenated. Then they are normalized with tf-idf transformation. For reducing the number of features and sparsity in n-gram vectors, tuples that have frequency less than 2 are ignored.

ab7f2a77-231a-436a-86eb-daef526ec5c6.label: text_chunk
ab7f2a77-231a-436a-86eb-daef526ec5c6.text: For character level n-gram N is selected as 3 , 4 , and 5 and for word level n-gram, N is 1 , 2 for Spanish and Arabic; 1 , 2 , 3 for English. The dimension of the vector is reduced by LSA to 300 . Then the vector is concatenated with neural representation which is produced right after tweet level attention in RNNwA model.

565f3efc-8325-44fd-8188-4c6b6a591b8f.label: text_chunk
565f3efc-8325-44fd-8188-4c6b6a591b8f.text: The resultant representation is fed to a fully- connected layer that produces predictions.

725d37f5-4179-4edd-8336-ed96bc051fe3.label: text_chunk
725d37f5-4179-4edd-8336-ed96bc051fe3.text:
2.4 Dataset

Models are tested on the PAN 2018 author profiling dataset (Pardo et al., 2018), which provides tweets in three languages: English, Spanish and Arabic with training/test datasets of sizes ( 3000 users, 1900 users), ( 3000 users, 2200 users), and ( 1500 users, 1000 users) respectively, where each user has 100 tweets.

a0d838f9-8f44-452f-9bd8-1cb119ae24b1.label: text_chunk
a0d838f9-8f44-452f-9bd8-1cb119ae24b1.text: Each training set is further partitioned randomly into training and validation sets with the ratio ( 0 . 8 , 0 . 2 ) respectively for hyper-parameter optimization.

3e4bb412-86fb-4514-b5da-6cedd38d4ec9.label: text_chunk
3e4bb412-86fb-4514-b5da-6cedd38d4ec9.text:
3 Results

In order to measure the effectiveness of the attention mechanism, in addition to the CNN base- line model (CNNwA) and RNNwA, two new models (denoted as CNN and RNN) are created by removing the tweet level attention layer (word level attention stays the same) and generating a pre- diction for each tweet then just simply taking an average to give a user level prediction.

a04d8155-c65a-4297-81aa-9f1762add3af.label: text_chunk
a04d8155-c65a-4297-81aa-9f1762add3af.text:
Tweet level accuracies for these models are shown in Table 1.

In Table 2, user level accuracy results for the proposed model (RNNwA) along with the baseline models are given. As can be seen in the results, tweet level attention mechanism increases the score of all baseline models with the only exception of the CNNwA model in Arabic.Also, compared to the best neural model (Takahashi et al.

924c870f-7996-4816-b3d0-2fe09d827004.label: text_chunk
924c870f-7996-4816-b3d0-2fe09d827004.text: , 2018) where max pooling is used instead of an attention mechanism on the outputs of RNN, the proposed model (RNNwA) gives better results in terms of accuracy on English and Arabic datasets, and produces similar accuracy levels on Spanish dataset (Table 3). These results show that an attention layer is able to learn ”where/how to look” for features that are helpful in identifying the gender of a user.

77fb50df-d2b0-4ac4-928c-00ab20e04c03.label: text_chunk
77fb50df-d2b0-4ac4-928c-00ab20e04c03.text: On the other hand, the improved model (RNNwA + n-gram), where neural and hand-crafted features are concatenated, increases the accuracy of the proposed model by approximately 0 , 5 % on English and approximately 2 % in Spanish and Arabic. This also supports our intuition that the performance of neural models can be improved by hand-crafted features, which is based on the study of Collobert and Weston (2008).

d35b421d-8b99-4979-bef0-a5f5e4579eb5.label: text_chunk
d35b421d-8b99-4979-bef0-a5f5e4579eb5.text:
As can be seen in Table 3, the improved model outperforms the state-of-the-art method of Daneshvar and Inkpen (2018) in English and produces competitive results in Spanish and Arabic.5

29315726-a1a2-4b0c-821c-c4253584a6af.text: During the hyperparameter optimization of the models RNN and CNN, we saved both the models that gave the best tweet-level accuracy and the models that gave the best user-level accuracy. The expectation is to see that the best setup on tweet-level also gives the best performance in user-level, but the outcome is the opposite: Best setups on tweet-level always fall behind best user-level setups.

91605220-8696-462a-921d-f49d4ac9b74f.label: text_chunk
91605220-8696-462a-921d-f49d4ac9b74f.text: Performance differences between various setups can be seen in Figure 3 where accuracies of the best three models in terms of tweet-level and best three models in terms of user-level are shown for all languages. It can be observed that the best tweet- level setups are almost 4% worse in terms of user-level accuracy. Deeper investigation shows that the best user-level models exhibit slight overfitting on tweet-level, in training.

c3783efb-8b71-4799-8a92-d8d17428a733.label: text_chunk
c3783efb-8b71-4799-8a92-d8d17428a733.text: Although overfitting normally leads to poor generalization, in this case we believe that this overfitting acts similar to an attention mechanism by over-emphasizing some important tweets and ignoring uninformative ones in the process. Even though this leads to poor tweet-level accuracy, it improves the user-level accuracy of the models as it can be seen from the Figure 3.Figure 3: Comparison of Tweet-Level and User-level accuracy of RNN Model.

4 Conclusion

In this work,

8661c585-74c6-4158-a146-94b084059589.label: text_chunk
8661c585-74c6-4158-a146-94b084059589.text: a neural network-based model namely RNN with attention (RNNwA) is proposed on the task of gender prediction from tweets. The proposed model is further improved by hand-crafted features which are obtained by LSA-reduced n-grams and concatenated with the neural represen- tation from RNNwA. User representations that is the result of this model is then fed to a fully- connected layer to make prediction.

1555ed9d-548d-4552-ba82-ef919ac9ae29.label: text_chunk
1555ed9d-548d-4552-ba82-ef919ac9ae29.text: This improved model achieved state-of-the-art accuracy on English and has a competitive performance on Spanish and Arabic.We also would like to kindly remind our readers that although the model is self-learning, there might still exist a gender bias in the evaluation of the model due to the data itself. Since the model learns to predict the gender directly from tweets of the twitter users, any bias the twitter users have might be reflected in the model predictions.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the interplay between overfitting, user-level models, attention mechanisms, filters, and accuracy in predictive modeling.

1. **Overfitting and User-level Models**: Overfitting negatively impacts user-level models by causing them to make inaccurate predictions. This occurs because the model may concentrate excessively on specific tweets, neglecting broader user behavior patterns.

2. **Overfitting and Attention Mechanism**: Overfitting is likened to an attention mechanism, as both prioritize significant tweets while disregarding less informative ones. This similarity underscores how overfitting can distort the model's focus, leading to potential misinterpretations of user behavior.

3. **Filters and Accuracy**: The number of filters employed in a model significantly affects its accuracy. Different configurations of filters can lead to varying performance levels, indicating that careful selection and tuning of filters are crucial for optimizing predictive outcomes.

In summary, the relationships emphasize the critical role of overfitting in shaping the effectiveness of user-level models and its analogy to attention mechanisms, alongside the importance of filter configurations in enhancing model accuracy.

The relationships outlined describe the interactions and transformations within a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) during the encoding phase. 

1. **Transforms -> W enc -> h t - 1**: The weight matrix \( W_{enc} \) plays a crucial role in transforming the input vector \( w_t \) along with the previous hidden state vector \( h_{t-1} \) into a new representation, facilitating the encoding process in the RNN.

2. **Transforms -> Matrix -> Sigmoid Function**: A matrix undergoes transformation through the sigmoid function, which generates gates that signify the importance of various inputs. This relationship highlights how the matrix acts as an input to the sigmoid function, emphasizing the creation of gating mechanisms that control information flow.

3. **W enc -> Transforms -> h t - 1**: This reiterates the function of the weight matrix \( W_{enc} \) in transforming both the current input vector \( w_t \) and the previous hidden state \( h_{t-1} \) into a new representation, underscoring its significance in the RNN's encoding phase.

4. **Matrix -> Transforms -> Sigmoid Function**: Similar to the previous point, this relationship emphasizes the transformation of the matrix by the sigmoid function, which is essential for generating gates that determine the relevance of inputs in the RNN architecture.

In summary, the weight matrix \( W_{enc} \) is pivotal in encoding inputs and previous states into new representations, while the transformation of matrices by the sigmoid function is crucial for creating gates that manage the importance of information within the RNN.
